title: podman pods with pasta network fail to start
body:
### Issue Description

When running a pod with --network pasta: it simply fails

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `podman pod create --replace --infra=true --hostname pod_main --network pasta:--mtu,1500,-a,x.y.z.0,-n,24,-g,x.y.z.2,--dns-forward,x.y.z.3,--no-ndp,--no-dhcpv6,--no-dhcp --share "net,ipc" --uts private main_pod` (Replace x.y.z with network of choice not already used)
2. podman pod start main_pod


### Describe the results you received

```
Error: starting container 0dbf397ea097b7b98e1214eff53ede7f925eafa9dea1217fc3a679a337f4b695: pasta failed with exit code 1:
Couldn't get any nameserver address
Template interface: enp35s0 (IPv4), enp35s0 (IPv6)
Namespace interface: enp35s0
MAC:
    host: a8:a1:[...]
DNS search list:
    example.com[replaced]
DNS search list:
    example.com[replaced]
Couldn't set IPv4 route(s) in guest: Network is unreachable
```

### Describe the results you expected

It working like it did with the previous pasta version in Fedora 38

### podman info output

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: Fedora 38 [update](https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/FEDORA-2023-3a7b730bc9) with fix should reach "testing" in a bit.

Given the fix is merged upstream I close this.

@sbrivio-rh @dgibson I invited you to this repo to give you triage permissions, with that you should be able to close/reopen issues and apply labels and assign people for example.

> Given the fix is merged upstream I close this.
> 
> @sbrivio-rh @dgibson I invited you to this repo to give you triage permissions, with that you should be able to close/reopen issues and apply labels and assign people for example.

Thanks @Luap99!

@waffshappen this is now fixed in [passt-0^20230823.ga7e4bfb-1.fc38](https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/FEDORA-2023-3a7b730bc9).

> @waffshappen this is now fixed in [passt-0^20230823.ga7e4bfb-1.fc38](https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/FEDORA-2023-3a7b730bc9).

Can confirm it is fixed 😃
@sbrivio-rh if RH plans to ship a newer version of passt in an EL9 update, will this fix be included? If yes, will there be cherrypicks only, or a release based on more recent commit?

> @sbrivio-rh if RH plans to ship a newer version of passt in an EL9 update, will this fix be included? If yes, will there be cherrypicks only, or a release based on more recent commit?

Also very interested to know this. I have crun 1.8.7 pinned (https://github.com/containers/crun/issues/1302) and built the f38 passt version for cos-9 due to https://passt.top/passt/commit/?id=5e4f7b92b0b0bf4724c505fa95fcae1526a8f88b
I believe we've been typically updating the release rather than cherry picking.  @sbrivio-rh would know better, but he's otherwise occupied most of this week.

You can see the sources for centos stream: https://gitlab.com/redhat/centos-stream/rpms/passt/-/tree/c9s?ref_type=heads
Looks like it set to 0^20230818.g0af928e currently which I assume will ship in RHEL 9.3 so it will likely not contain this bug fix.

In general if you need fixes in RHEL or Centos Stream you have to file bug reports against the distro version. Upstream cannot really help with that.

We can hope only hope these issues if present somewhere are not locked by redhat, because otherwise its not easy to write a meaningful bug report - thats why it seems easier to ask redhat insider directly, sadly..

> Looks like it set to 0^20230818.g0af928e currently which I assume will ship in RHEL 9.3 so it will likely not contain this bug fix.

that's exactly why I dared to ask Stefano here
